texto: You know, Dorothy, you can’t let people bring you down so easily or you’ll have your nose in the dirt for the rest of your life. From what I make of it, for every person with a good thought, there are about fifty who’d try to spoil it. We have to guard our good ideas, our happy thoughts, and fight for them. Because if we let those others snuff them out, well, we didn’t after all deserve them.
explicacao: Orientação direta de J.M. Lavallee sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “You know, Dorothy, you can’t let people bring you down so easily or you’ll have your nose in the …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, canada, canadian, útil em momentos de canadian e canadian-literature.
